summary: BABA Q4-FY26: revenue RMB 243B, cloud +40% YoY with AI 30% of cloud revenue. MCS 0.77 (1B/3H/1M).
quotes: Cloud Intelligence Group's external revenue growth accelerated to 40%, with AI-related products accounting for 30% of this revenue.
Customer management revenue grew 8% on a like-for-like basis. We are confident in our business outlook and will continue to invest in AI + Cloud.
AIDC narrowed loss significantly year-over-year, approaching break-even.
Income from operations was RMB10,645 million, a decrease of 74% year-over-year, primarily due to impairment of goodwill.
bull: Q4-FY26 cloud +40% YoY with AI 30% of cloud — re-acceleration confirmed
CMR +8% LFL — take-rate stabilizing after years of compression
Net cash $16.8B — largest in eCommerce comp set, funds buyback
MCS 0.77 on 5 closed claims — 1 beats, 3 hits, 1 miss
bear: Q4-FY26 revenue only +2.9% YoY — modest growth
Q2/Q3-FY26 goodwill impairments compressed op margins to 2-4%
FY26 FCF outflow RMB 47B on AI/cloud capex acceleration
EV/EBITDA premium vs peer average — vulnerable if cloud re-decelerates
PRC platform regulation + PCAOB review structural risks
JD at P/E 10x — BABA premium could compress on growth scare
bottom_line: BABA at $137.3 (DD -28.74% from 52w high). FactSet target $191.95 (+44.8%). Q4-FY26 validated cloud re-acceleration (+40% YoY, AI 30% of cloud). MCS 0.77 (1B/3H/1M). Stance: NEUTRAL with bias to add on weakness toward 200-DMA.
disclaimer: Institutional research support. Not personalized investment advice. Foreign private issuer (Cayman Islands ADR) — financials in RMB.
